title: api.ts: replace ~250 hand-rolled fetch blocks with get/post/put/patch/del verb helpers
description: Nearly every one of the ~140 members in the api object repeats the same 4-6 line incantation: `apiFetch(`${API_BASE}/...`, { method, headers: getHeaders(), body: JSON.stringify(x) }).then(handleResponse)`. Compare api.ts:103-104 (GET), 156-160 (POST no body), 396-401 (POST with body), 461-466 (PUT), 270-274 (PATCH), 276-280 (DELETE) — identical scaffolding hundreds of times over 1100 lines. The only variations are: long timeout (`signal: AbortSignal.timeout(LONG_TIMEOUT_MS)`, e.g. 140, 176, 202) and three unauthenticated endpoints that pass a bare Content-Type header (login:44-49, OAuth callbacks:67-72/81-86/95-100, submitContact:1035-1040). Also inconsistent: getHealth (40-41) uses `.then(res => res.json())` instead of handleResponse, silently skipping error mapping.
impact: ~550-600 lines removed (1132 → ~500); every endpoint reads as method+path+payload at a glance; long-timeout and public-endpoint exceptions become explicit flags instead of buried option diffs
guidance: Four corrections/traps: (1) Seven GETs currently send NO headers at all — getHealth (40-41), googleStatus (62), googleAuthUrl (64-65), microsoftStatus (76), microsoftAuthUrl (78-79), githubAuthStatus (89-90), githubAuthUrl (92-93). The sketched `get` always sends getHeaders() (adds Content-Type + Authorization when a token exists); give `get` the same auth:false escape (sending no headers) or leave those 7 raw to preserve behavior on these public routes. (2) Do NOT silently convert getHealth to handleResponse as part of the dedup: /api/health (api/src/index.ts:276-282) always returns 200 and App.tsx:403-410 try/catches it, so the change is practically harmless, but it alters error type/resolution semantics — make it a separate deliberate change or keep res.json(). (3) The bonus delegation is a landmine: inside the api object literal, bare `updateTask(...)` resolves to the module-level export at line 1072 (PUT /tasks/:taskId, board-level), NOT api.updateTask at 324 (PATCH /agents/:agentId/tasks/:taskId); arrow members have no usable `this`. Delegate via api.updateTask(...) or a distinctly named local helper (e.g. patchAgentTask), and keep updateTaskRepo/updateTaskStorage's null-normalization (lines 335, 342) in the wrappers. (4) There are zero frontend tests, so tsc cannot catch a wrong verb or typo'd path string across ~150 entries — do the transformation in small reviewable chunks (or script it) and diff carefully. Minor: realistic line savings is ~400-500, not 550-600, because ~25 query-string-building GETs (488-601, 887-977) only shrink by one line each.

title: api.ts: generate the 10 copy-pasted per-provider integration endpoint trios from a factory
description: The agentId/boardId → querystring builder is copy-pasted 15 times verbatim (lines 488-494, 496-503, 512-518, 520-526, 535-541, 543-549, 558-564, 566-572, 581-587, 589-595, 887-893, 910-916, 933-939, 956-962, 964-970) and the `{ ...(agentId && { agentId }), ...(boardId && { boardId }) }` disconnect body 9 times (508, 531, 554, 577, 600, 906, 929, 952, 976). OneDrive/Gmail/Outlook/GDrive/Slack/GitHub each define an identical status/auth-url/disconnect trio differing only in the path segment; Jira/WordPress/S3 share the status/connect/disconnect shape. The login-OAuth trio (google 61-72, microsoft 75-86, github 89-100) is a third identical pattern. Adding the next provider today means pasting 25 lines and changing one word.
impact: ~160 lines → ~45 in the integrations section plus ~30 → ~10 for login OAuth; the next provider becomes a 4-line addition; eliminates the chance of the query/body builders drifting apart
guidance: Four behavior-preservation traps and one description correction: (1) The sketch's `get`/`post` helpers do not exist in api.ts — implement the factory with the existing apiFetch/getHeaders/handleResponse primitives, or add those helpers first; do not change timeout or response handling. (2) Header asymmetry: the login-OAuth GETs (lines 61-65, 75-79, 89-93, paths /auth/{provider}/status|url) send NO headers at all, and the callback POSTs send only Content-Type (no Authorization), whereas all integration endpoints send getHeaders(). The loginProvider factory must reproduce this exactly — do not add getHeaders() to login routes. (3) GitHub appears in BOTH families: login trio githubAuthStatus/githubAuthUrl/githubAuthCallback (/auth/github/*) and integration trio getGitHubStatus/getGitHubAuthUrl/disconnectGitHub (/github/*); both sets of flat names must survive. (4) Keep abBody's key order agentId-then-boardId as sketched (matches all 9 originals byte-for-byte). Correction to the description: Jira/WordPress/S3 do NOT share a connect shape — connectJira (895-900: domain/email/apiToken), connectWordPress (918-923: siteUrl/username/applicationPassword), connectS3 (941-946: accessKeyId/secretAccessKey/region/endpoint) all differ; only their status + disconnect pairs are factory-able, keep the three connect functions bespoke. Also note OneDrive's status (488-494) and disconnect (505-509) fit the standard factory — only its authUrl (496-503, consumer flag) needs to stay custom, as the sketch already says.

title: api.ts: delete 28 unused endpoint wrappers (verified zero callers)
description: An exhaustive grep over frontend/src (with no dynamic `api[...]` indexing anywhere in the codebase) shows these api members are never referenced outside api.ts: getAgent (115), getAgentStatus (118), getSwarmStatus (121), getAgentStatuses (124), getAgentsByProject (129), getProjectSummary (132), updateAllProjects (246), toggleTask (270), clearTasks (282), transferTask (288), updateTaskText (317), addTaskCommit (345), clearActionLogs (359), setPluginShared (416), addPluginMcp (424), removePluginMcp (430), createMcpServer (454), updateMcpServer (461), deleteMcpServer (468), githubAuthStatus (89), getBoard (791), updateBoardMcpAuth (835), getBoardAuditLogs (867), getTasksByAssignee (870), getBoardRepos (685), getBoardStorages (689), plus standalone exports bulkMoveTasks (1100-1105) and getBoards (1126-1127). The standalone getBoards duplicates api.getBoards (785-786) byte-for-byte and its comment '(standalone export for TaskModal)' is stale — no TaskModal imports it; all 8 consumers use api.getBoards.
sketch: Delete each listed member after a per-name confirmation grep (the API server keeps its routes — this only removes the unused client wrappers). Remove the stale '/* Boards (standalone export for TaskModal) */' block at 1125-1127 entirely. If some are kept deliberately as API surface, group them under a clearly-labeled '// currently unused — kept for parity with server routes' section instead of interleaving them with live code.
impact: ~140 lines removed; readers stop wondering which of two same-named exports (getBoards, updateTask, deleteTask) is the live one
guidance: Three corrections. (1) The impact claim 'readers stop wondering which of two same-named exports (getBoards, updateTask, deleteTask) is the live one' is overstated: this deletion list only resolves the getBoards pair. api.updateTask (api.ts:324, agent-scoped /agents/:id/tasks/:taskId) and standalone updateTask (api.ts:1072, /tasks/:id) are BOTH live and are different endpoints — do NOT delete api.updateTask (4 callers in frontend/src/components/tasks/TaskDetailModal.tsx:113,182,666,820). Consider a one-line comment distinguishing the two routes instead. (2) Bonus: api.deleteTask (api.ts:276, agent-scoped) has zero api.deleteTask( callers and can be added to the deletion list after the same per-name grep; the live one is standalone deleteTask (api.ts:1079), imported as deleteTaskById in TasksBoard.tsx. (3) Reject the sketch's fallback option of keeping members under a 'kept for parity with server routes' section — plain deletion is strictly better; wrappers are trivially re-added if a route gains a consumer. Trivial nit: 'all 8 consumers use api.getBoards' is actually 7 call sites across 6 files.

title: VoiceSessionContext: unify the three near-identical pending-result handlers (delegate/ask/management)
description: handleDelegation (268-329), handleAsk (331-392) and handleManagement (394-442) are three copies of the same 60-line ritual: guard missing socket/agent, build a `pending` record, define `settle()`, register a result handler filtered by agentId, arm a timeout that checks `activeAgentIdRef`, add to pendingResultsRef, then `sock.on(...)` + `sock.emit(...)`. delegate and ask differ only in event names, payload field, and message strings; management additionally matches on functionName and skips the DELEGATING status. This is the most delicate logic in the file (listener leaks, stale-agent timeouts) and it exists in triplicate — a fix to one copy (like the pendingResultsRef bookkeeping) must be hand-mirrored to the other two.
impact: ~175 lines → ~80; the settle/timeout/stale-agent invariants live in exactly one place
guidance: Four corrections to the sketch, all required for strict behavior preservation: (1) ORDERING — drop onStart from the helper. In all three originals the UI pre-work (setStatus(DELEGATING)/setDelegationTarget/setCurrentFunction/addEvent at 276-279, 339-342, 395-396) runs BEFORE the socket/agentId guard (283-286, 346-349, 400-403); a missing socket today leaves status=DELEGATING and delegationTarget set. The sketch guards first and calls onStart after, silently fixing that quirk. Keep the pre-work (and the missing-field guards at 268-274/331-337) inline in each wrapper before calling the helper. (2) SUCCESS vs TIMEOUT STATUS — success handlers do unconditional setStatus(STATUS.CONNECTED) (302, 365) while timeouts do conditional setStatus(prev => prev === DELEGATING ? CONNECTED : prev) (320, 383); management does neither. The single onSettle?.(data)/onSettle?.(null) callback must branch on its argument to reproduce both, or use two callbacks (onResult/onTimeout). (3) TIMEOUT STRINGS — originals use three distinct strings per flow (setCurrentFunction 321/384/434, addEvent 322/385/435, sendFunctionOutput 323/386/436); the sketch reuses one describe.timedOut for the first two. describe needs timedOutStatus/timedOutEvent/timedOutOutput separately. (4) STRING COERCION — delegate/ask call resultText.slice(0,200) directly (308, 371) while management uses String(resultText).slice (423); the sketch's String(text) everywhere changes the non-string-result path for delegate/ask (today it would throw). Either keep per-wrapper formatting of the addEvent line or accept and note the (benign) difference. Invariant to respect: the pending record must remain exactly { sock, event, handler, timer } because cleanupConnection destructures those fields (141-145). Also: register the handler with sock.on before sock.emit, as the originals do, and keep helper deps at [addEvent, sendFunctionOutput] so handleToolCall's dep list stays correct.

title: App.tsx: extract agents/stream socket wiring into a useAgentsSocket hook
description: App.tsx interleaves two unrelated concerns: auth/session lifecycle and the agent-streaming socket protocol. The protocol half — agents/thinkingMap/streamBuffers state (19-31), the stale-thinking sweep effect (166-179), SOCKET_EVENTS (185-190, recreated every render since it lives in the component body) and the 165-line initSocket (192-358) with its STREAM_START/CHUNK/END/RESUME/ERROR choreography and the streamEndedAgents/activeStreamAgents ref dance — is self-contained and carries the file's densest comments (159-165, 272-276, 301-304). Readers tracing a login bug must scroll past all of it, and vice versa.
impact: App.tsx 651 → ~430 lines; the stream-buffer protocol (the part with the subtlest ordering invariants) gets its own reviewable module; fixes the per-render SOCKET_EVENTS allocation
guidance: Corrections and traps: (1) The description's "agents/thinkingMap/streamBuffers state (19-31)" over-claims — lines 20-23 (templates/projects/skills/mcpServers) are unrelated data state that stays in App; only lines 19, 24-31 move. (2) activeStreamAgents (App.tsx:30, written at 260/265/280/295/320) is WRITE-ONLY — nothing ever calls .has on it, contradicting its own comment ("source of truth for whether to keep a streamBuffer alive"). Move it verbatim; deleting it or wiring it up is a separate dead-code finding, not part of this behavior-preserving split. Same for the no-op eviction loop in STREAM_RESUME (285-289, empty body) — move as-is. (3) In App, declare showToast/showToastRef BEFORE the useAgentsSocket call (showToastRef currently sits at line 182, after the stream state); the ref-sync effect stays in App since the window-event effect at 495-511 also uses showToastRef. (4) Split the verify-effect cleanup correctly: `cancelled = true` stays in App; only the SOCKET_EVENTS.forEach(sock.off) loop becomes hook.teardown(). Keep the hook's initSocket on [] deps so App's [initSocket, loadData] effect still runs exactly once. (5) Carry the load-bearing comments (159-165, 272-276, 301-304) into the new file — they document the ordering invariants that motivated this extraction. (6) frontend/src/hooks/ does not exist yet; create it. The new file has no JSX so .ts is fine.

title: App.tsx: consolidate the four copy-pasted login completion flows and the OAuth path switch
description: The user-object construction from an auth payload is copy-pasted four times (368-376 verify, 445-452 OAuth callback, 471-478 handleLogin, 518-524 handleImpersonate) — the impersonate copy already silently dropped termsAcceptedAt/tutorialCompletedAt, showing the copies are drifting. The post-login sequence `loadData(); initSocket(token); checkDbHealth()` repeats three times (377-380, 453-455, 479-481). The OAuth callback effect (421-439) has three branches identical except for path and api function. Finally, `googleLoading` (33) is misleadingly named: it is also set for Microsoft (428) and GitHub (434) logins.
impact: ~55 lines removed; adding a fourth login provider becomes a one-line table entry; the impersonate drift bug class disappears
guidance: 1) Drop the "impersonate drift bug" framing: the backend impersonate response (api/src/middleware/auth.ts:224-231) intentionally omits termsAcceptedAt/tutorialCompletedAt, and App.tsx:636 gates the welcome modal on !user.impersonatedBy, so nothing is broken today. Applying toUser there is still safe (fields become null; gate short-circuits), but it is unification, not a bug fix. 2) Do NOT route the initial-verify effect (App.tsx:360-401) through completeLogin: it has a load-bearing `if (cancelled) return;` at line 378 between `await loadData()` and `initSocket(token)` (StrictMode/unmount guard), and it must not be lost. Use only toUser there, or thread an isCancelled callback. 3) checkDbHealth asymmetry: the OAuth path awaits it (455) so the .finally's setLoading(false) runs after the health result; handleLogin (481) does not await. For strict preservation, have completeLogin return the checkDbHealth promise and await it only at the OAuth call site (or accept a one-request spinner-timing shift and document it). 4) handleImpersonate (513-528) must not use completeLogin — it disconnects the socket before initSocket, never calls checkDbHealth, and does not await loadData; apply only toUser there. 5) The rename also touches frontend/src/components/LoginPage.tsx:553-556 (prop name in signature/type) and line 858 — add LoginPage.tsx to the files list. 6) Type the toUser parameter loosely (the verify path feeds data.user, the others feed the flat response) or accept both shapes explicitly; the field names are identical across all payloads, verified against the backend.

title: App.tsx: one delete-key reducer instead of nine inline copy/delete blocks
description: The pattern `setX(prev => { if (!(id in prev)) return prev; const copy = { ...prev }; delete copy[id]; return copy; })` is written out nine times: thinkingMap at 168-178 (loop variant), 213-219, 237-243, 248-253, 296-300, 332-337; streamBuffers at 224-229, 308-314, 338-342. Each occurrence is 5-7 lines of noise around a one-word intent ('clear this agent's entry').
impact: ~45 lines → ~12; socket handlers shrink to their actual intent (pairs naturally with the useAgentsSocket extraction)
guidance: Sites are NOT all identical as described — three variants exist. (1) Guarded copy/delete at 213-219, 237-243, 248-253, 332-337; (2) UNGUARDED copy/delete at 224-229, 296-300, 308-314, 338-342; (3) loop sweep with `changed` flag at 168-178. Standardize on the GUARDED helper as sketched: `if (!(key in obj)) return obj;`. Do NOT drop the guard — that would regress the four guarded sites with wasted re-renders on hot AGENT_STATUS/AGENT_UPDATED events. Adding the guard to the four unguarded sites only skips no-op re-renders (identical UI); safe. For the sweep effect (166-179), fold per key: `let next = prev; for (const id of Object.keys(prev)) if (!busyIds.has(id)) next = withoutKey(next, id); return next;` — this preserves the existing `changed ? copy : prev` identity bailout exactly because withoutKey returns the same reference when the key is absent. Keep all surrounding logic untouched: streamEndedAgents.current.has/delete at 222-223 and 307-308, the setTimeout at 306-315, status!=='busy' checks, and the else branch of AGENT_THINKING (255). Define withoutKey at module scope (file is TSX — type it `<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(obj: T, key: string): T`); clearThinking/clearStreamBuffer are safe inside the deps-free initSocket useCallback because setState setters are stable.

title: VoiceSessionContext: drop dead context aliases (agentId, isConnected) and unify muted/isMuted
description: The context value exposes `activeAgentId` plus an alias `agentId: activeAgentId` (916-917) and `muted` plus `isMuted: muted` (918-919), and computes `isConnected` (933). Grep shows `agentId` and `isConnected` have zero consumers — vestiges of the pre-purge API. `isMuted` is consumed only by VoiceChat.tsx (25, 97-101) while VoiceChatTab.tsx and ActiveVoiceIndicator.tsx use `muted` — two names for one value forces readers to confirm they are the same thing.
sketch: In the value memo: remove `agentId: activeAgentId`, `isMuted: muted`, and the `isConnected` line (its expression duplicates isActive minus CONNECTING and nothing reads it). In VoiceChat.tsx replace the `isMuted` destructure and its three usages with `muted`.
impact: 3 dead lines + one alias removed; the context surface matches what is actually consumed
guidance: Two corrections. (1) VoiceChat.tsx is itself dead: grep shows nothing imports its default export — AgentDetail.tsx imports only VoiceChatTab and ExternalVoiceChatTab. Prefer deleting frontend/src/components/VoiceChat.tsx entirely instead of renaming isMuted→muted inside it; that makes the isMuted alias zero-consumer by construction. If the owner wants to keep the file for a future tab, the sketched rename is also safe as written. (2) The rationale's claim that isConnected "duplicates isActive minus CONNECTING" is backwards-ish: isActive (line 907) = status !== DISCONNECTED && status !== ERROR, while isConnected additionally excludes CONNECTING, so they are NOT equivalent — deletion is still safe (zero consumers) but do not justify it as a duplicate of isActive. No traps otherwise: the useMemo dependency array (lines 935-953) already omits the aliases, so no dep edits are needed; keep `muted` in the deps.

title: App.tsx: remove the dead no-op eviction loop in the STREAM_RESUME handler
description: In the STREAM_RESUME handler (281-292), the loop `for (const id of Object.keys(prev)) { if (!activeIds.has(id) && !(id in next)) { /* intentionally omit — buffer gets evicted */ } }` has an empty body. Eviction already happens by construction because `next` is built fresh from the server list; the loop iterates and does nothing. Worse, its comment implies it performs the eviction, sending readers hunting for a side effect that does not exist.
impact: 8 misleading lines → 2 accurate ones in the trickiest reconnect path
guidance: Sketch is correct as written. Two notes: (1) do NOT 'fix' the eviction to spare buffers for agents in streamEndedAgents.current (the anti-flash mechanism at lines 301-315) — current behavior drops them on STREAM_RESUME and must stay that way; (2) the header comment at lines 272-276 ('...AND evict any local buffers...') remains accurate after the change (eviction by construction) and can be left untouched.

title: VoiceSessionContext: single resetSessionState helper for the four state-reset litanies
description: The same 8-10 setter litany (setStatus, setActiveAgentId + activeAgentIdRef, setMuted, setError, setDelegationTarget, setCurrentTranscript, setCurrentResponse, setCurrentFunction, setEvents) is repeated with slight variations in disconnect (811-823), reconnect (825-846), the socket-loss effect (884-899), and the connect error path (798-806). Spotting which variant resets which fields (reconnect clears events, disconnect does not; the error path keeps activeAgentId null but sets error text) takes a diff in your head.
impact: ~50 lines → ~20; the deliberate differences between the four resets become explicit named options instead of omissions
guidance: Two behavior-preservation traps in the sketch: (1) reconnect (lines 825-846) deliberately does NOT null setActiveAgentId/activeAgentIdRef — the sketched helper nulls both unconditionally, which during the 100ms setTimeout window flips VoiceChatTab.tsx:7-8 (isSessionForAgent/isOtherAgentActive) and ActiveVoiceIndicator.tsx:5-14 rendering, and changes the gating of the effects at lines 873-882 and 884-899. Add a keepAgent: true option and pass it from reconnect. (2) The connect catch path (798-806) does NOT setMuted(false) — a user can toggle mute after the mic stream attaches (line 588) but before SDP completes, and current code preserves muted=true on failure; the helper must not reset muted on that path (keepMuted option, or have the helper skip muted by default and only the other three sites reset it). Safe non-issues: setDelegationTarget(null) added to the catch path is a no-op (already null from line 562, dc messages cannot interleave with connect's sync tail, React bails out on same-value sets); setter ordering differences across sites are unobservable under React 18 automatic batching; the helper's useCallback with [] deps is correct (setters and refs are stable). Keep addEvent calls OUTSIDE the helper (disconnect: addEvent('system','Session ended'); catch: addEvent('error', msg); reconnect and socket-loss: no event). Do NOT fold in the fifth litany at connect() start (560-569) — it has a different shape (CONNECTING, sets agentId, clears events) and folding it would over-parameterize the helper.

title: Use lib/safeStorage everywhere instead of three re-implemented try/catch localStorage wrappers
description: lib/safeStorage.ts exists precisely for storage-blocked browsers, yet the same try/catch is re-implemented inline in api.ts getHeaders (15-16), socket.ts auth callback (28-29), and LanguageContext.tsx which redefines its own private safeGet/safeSet pair with the identical comment (14-20). App.tsx already imports the lib version — the other three predate it.
sketch:
api.ts: import { safeGet } from './lib/safeStorage'; getHeaders uses `const token = safeGet('token')`.
socket.ts: same for the auth callback.
LanguageContext.tsx: delete the local safeGet/safeSet (lines 13-20) and import from '../lib/safeStorage'.
impact: ~12 duplicated lines removed across 3 files; one canonical place documents the storage-blocked rationale
guidance: Sketch is correct as written. Two minor notes: (1) LanguageContext.tsx lines 13-14 carry a provider-specific rationale ("this provider wraps the whole app, so degrade instead of crashing the tree") not present in the lib header — optionally keep that single comment line near the import; the lib header at safeStorage.ts:1-3 covers the general rationale either way. (2) The proposal's claim that only App.tsx imports the lib understates it — ThemeContext, Dashboard, TasksBoard, and AgentDetail do too, which strengthens the case. socket.ts:56's comment referencing localStorage remains accurate after the swap; leave it.
